title: Teaching & Outreach
text: I care about creating welcoming spaces where people can explore geoscience, develop technical skills, and communicate ideas with confidence.

title: Teaching & Outreach
text:
Teaching and mentoring
My teaching emphasizes curiosity, clear communication, practical problem-solving, and an inclusive learning environment.
Courses, workshops, and mentoring activities will be added soon.

title: Teaching & Outreach
text:
Outreach and science communication
I enjoy making geoscience accessible through public engagement, bilingual communication, and creative approaches—including lessons learned through improvisational theater.
Selected outreach projects will be added soon.

title: About
text:
I am a tectonic geomorphologist from Santiago, Chile. I study how tectonics, climate, erosion, and sediment transport shape landscapes across timescales.
My work combines field geology, remote sensing, geochronology, thermochronology, and numerical modeling.
I also care deeply about inclusive geoscience, teaching, mentoring, outreach, and science communication—including lessons learned through improvisational theater.
More biography and a portrait will be added soon.

title: Gallery
text: A curated collection of fieldwork, laboratory work, landscapes, teaching, and life outside science is coming soon.

title: Tools & Code

Remote sensing
Sentinel-1 InSAR, MintPy, GNSS integration, GIS, and DEM analysis.

AHe, ZHe, AFT, HeFTy modeling, and luminescence sampling.

title: Tools & Code

Python, MATLAB, PyGMT, TopoToolbox, and scientific visualization.
Selected workflows and public repositories will be added soon.

title: Research
text: My research asks how landscapes record interacting tectonic and climatic processes.

title: Research
text:
Faults, earthquakes, and landscape response
Slowly slipping faults can leave measurable records in topography, drainage networks, sediment deposits, and surface deformation.

title: Research
text:
Climate variability and sediment transport
I use landscape-evolution models to examine how climate oscillations and sediment cover influence rivers, drainage divides, and fault-zone morphology.

title: Research
text:
Thermochronology and exhumation
I use low-temperature thermochronology and geologic constraints to reconstruct cooling, exhumation, and basin development.

title: Research
text:
Glacial and postglacial landscapes
My developing research expands these questions into mountain systems shaped by ice, rivers, tectonics, and changing sediment flux.

title: Tamara Aránguiz-Rago
text:
I am a tectonic geomorphologist interested in how faults, climate, erosion, and sediment transport shape Earth’s surface across timescales.
My research combines field observations, remote sensing, geochronology, thermochronology, and numerical modeling to understand how landscapes respond to tectonic and climatic forcing.

title: Tamara Aránguiz-Rago
text:
Research themes



Active faults and landscape response
I study how slowly slipping faults leave measurable records in topography, drainage networks, sediment deposits, and surface deformation.




Climate, sediment, and landscape evolution
I use numerical models to test how climate variability and sediment cover influence river dynamics, divide migration, and fault-zone morphology.




Long-term exhumation and mountain evolution
I use thermochronology and geologic constraints to reconstruct cooling, exhumation, and landscape development over millions of years.

title: Tamara Aránguiz-Rago
text:
Current direction
After completing my Ph.D. research in the hyperarid Atacama Desert, I am expanding my work toward glaciated and postglacial landscapes, sediment routing, and tectonic geomorphology in mountain systems.
